commands.repair_reason: Not a repair-lane candidate: the patch is mechanically sound and the remaining blocker is contributor or maintainer real behavior proof, not an automation-fixable code defect.; Cleared: The diff only changes agent harness selection code and colocated tests; I found no concrete security or supply-chain regression. commands.review_summary:
The PR updates agent harness selection so explicit CLI runtime aliases and configured CLI backend ids return the PI placeholder path instead of throwing, with focused selection tests for aliases, configured backends, and unknown runtimes.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection of current main shows an explicit non-auto runtime override such as claude-cli reaches the forced-harness branch and throws MissingAgentHarnessError unless a matching plugin harness exists; I did not run a write-producing test in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: Not a repair-lane candidate: the patch is mechanically sound and the remaining blocker is contributor or maintainer real behavior proof, not an automation-fixable code defect.

target.title: fix(agents/harness): pass CLI runtime aliases through to PI in selectAgentHarnessDecision
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes the `MissingAgentHarnessError: Requested agent harness "claude-cli" is not registered.` throw in `selectAgentHarnessDecision()` (`src/agents/harness/selection.ts`) when a model's `agentRuntime.id` is set to a CLI runtime alias (`claude-cli`, `google-gemini-cli`) or a configured `cliBackends` id.

The explicit-non-`auto` branch currently fail-closes for any runtime that has no registered plugin harness counterpart. Model dispatch is unaffected (the CLI-runtime short-circuit in `assertModelFallbackCandidateHarnessAvailable` runs first), but every non-dispatch caller — delivery-mirror metadata lookups, lane preflight, channel projection — surfaces the throw. On Slack `[[reply_to:]]` deliveries the warning text gets substituted into the assistant message synthesized as `provider: openclaw, model: gateway-injected`, poisoning the thread.

The fix mirrors the existing implicit-codex escape hatch in the same function: when the runtime is a CLI alias (`isCliRuntimeAlias`) or a configured CLI backend (`isCliProvider`), return PI with the new `selectedReason: "cli_runtime_passthrough_pi"`. Non-CLI typos still throw as before. New tests cover the canonical alias set, the configured `cliBackends` path, and the preserved throw for unknown runtimes.

Refs #85582.

## Verification

Behavior addressed: explicit `agentRuntime.id` of a CLI runtime alias or configured cliBackends id no longer throws `MissingAgentHarnessError` from `selectAgentHarnessDecision()` on non-dispatch call paths; unknown non-CLI runtimes still throw.

Real environment tested: macOS arm64, Node 22.22.2, pnpm 11.2.2 via corepack. OpenClaw v2026.5.20 install on Proxmox LXC (Debian 12, Node 24.13.0) confirmed the unpatched behavior by log inspection prior to the fix (every 1–5 min `lane task error: MissingAgentHarnessError` against both Slack and dashboard lanes on a `claude-cli` model).

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
- `pnpm install`
- `pnpm test src/agents/harness/selection.test.ts`
- `pnpm check:changed`

Evidence after fix: `pnpm test src/agents/harness/selection.test.ts` reports `1 passed | 32 tests`; `pnpm check:changed` exits 0 with typecheck, lint, import cycles, and the auth/webhook/pairing guards all green.

Observed result after fix: in production, after applying an equivalent bundle-level patch on the v2026.5.20 install (mirrors this source change with the same control-flow shape), `MissingAgentHarnessError` log entries dropped from one every 1–5 min to zero over 40+ minutes since restart. No regressions observed in agent dispatch, dashboard chat, or other runtime paths.

What was not tested: Slack `[[reply_to:]]` end-to-end delivery is not verified yet (the bundle patch stops the throw, but a fresh Slack interaction is needed to confirm the actual reply text now lands; will follow up in #85582 if the next Slack thread confirms it). Full `pnpm check` + `pnpm test:changed` against latest `origin/main` not run from this branch (scoped `pnpm check:changed` was used).
target.author: potterdigital
